title: Daily Market Digest: Geopolitical Tensions & Weak Jobs Data Drive Risk-Off Sentiment
subtitle: Closing Bell: March 7, 2026 (Weekend Edition)

items.headline: Global Markets End Week in Risk-Off Environment Amid Oil Spike and Weak Jobs Report
items.summary: The week concluded with a palpable risk-off sentiment across global markets, heavily influenced by a sudden surge in oil prices due to escalating Middle East tensions and a surprisingly weak U.S. employment report. Major indices finished near their session lows on Friday, capping a challenging week for investors. The VIX fear gauge climbed to 29.49, marking a 70% increase over the past month and reflecting genuine investor unease. This digest reflects market activity for the trading day ending Friday, March 7, 2026. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Plunged 3.0% for the week, erasing all 2026 gains.
Led declines with a 4.1% weekly fall.
Up 70% in the past month, signaling investor unease.

items.headline: The Trade Desk Surges on CEO Buy and Partnership Speculation
items.summary: The Trade Desk ($TTD) jumped 22.9% for the week following CEO Jeff Green's substantial $148 million stock purchase and circulating reports of potential advertising partnership talks with OpenAI.

items.headline: CrowdStrike Achieves First GAAP Profit Amid Geopolitical Focus
items.summary: CrowdStrike ($CRWD) gained 15.3% for the week after reporting its first-ever positive GAAP net income of $38.69 million in Q4 FY2026, alongside 23% year-over-year revenue growth to $1.305 billion. Geopolitical tailwinds also highlighted cybersecurity stocks.

items.headline: Palantir Rides Defense-Tech Wave
items.summary: Palantir Technologies ($PLTR) saw a 14.6% increase, largely driven by geopolitical developments and increased focus on defense technology.

items.headline: Sable Offshore Corp. Leads Monthly Gainers
items.summary: Sable Offshore Corp. ($SOC) was a significant gainer, up 67.09% for March 2026, contributing to the energy sector's resilience.

items.headline: Rocket Companies Slides After AI Partnership News
items.summary: Rocket Companies ($RKT) shed 17.81% of its value following news that Better.com partnered with OpenAI, which may impact its mortgage lending business.

items.headline: Nomura Holdings Hit by Middle East Conflict Volatility
items.summary: Japanese banking giant Nomura Holdings ($NMR) retreated 14.56%, as the Japanese stock market was significantly impacted by volatility stemming from the Middle East conflict.

items.headline: Sherwin-Williams Under Pressure as Consumer Staples Falter
items.summary: Sherwin-Williams ($SHW) declined 9.02% as consumer staples stocks, often considered safe havens, came under pressure during the week.

items.headline: Oil Prices Surge, Fueling Stagflation Fears
items.summary: Crude oil saw its largest weekly percentage jump on record, surging $23.80 per barrel or 35.5%, finishing the week near $91. This spike is attributed to intensifying conflict in the Middle East and threats to shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns about transportation costs, corporate profit margins, and a potential resurgence of inflation, leading to 'stagflation' worries.

items.headline: U.S. Economy Lost 92,000 Jobs in February, Unemployment Rises to 4.4%
items.summary: The U.S. labor market showed significant weakness in February, with the economy losing 92,000 jobs and the unemployment rate rising to 4.4%. This unexpectedly weak employment data is a primary concern, signaling a potential economic slowdown.

items.headline: Fed Faces Paralysis Amid Conflicting Economic Signals
items.summary: Federal Reserve officials are grappling with conflicting signals: rising inflation concerns from the oil price spike versus a deteriorating jobs market. This dilemma suggests the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) may be 'utterly paralyzed' at its upcoming March 18th meeting regarding interest rate decisions. Meanwhile, Kevin Warsh has been nominated as the new Fed Chair, potentially succeeding Jerome Powell.

items.headline: Sector Rotation Reveals Investor Caution: Energy Leads, Staples and Financials Lag
items.summary: Sector performance this week highlighted a clear shift in investor positioning. Energy was one of the few sectors to hold up due to surging oil prices. In contrast, sectors sensitive to fuel costs and economic uncertainty, such as Materials, Health Care, Industrials, and Consumer Staples, saw significant declines. Financials also extended losses for the second consecutive week.

items.headline: HPE, Oracle, and Adobe Earnings Eyed Next Week
items.summary: Next week brings key earnings reports from major tech companies. Hewlett Packard Enterprise ($HPE) will reveal whether its Juniper Networks acquisition is delivering, while Oracle ($ORCL) faces scrutiny over its cloud revenue growth versus a prior EPS beat and revenue miss. Adobe ($ADBE) will be watched for how its AI features are driving growth.
